tasks.title: Cancel Xfinity internet
tasks.goal: Cancel home internet service before April 5 without accepting an overpriced retention offer.
tasks.next_action: Approve or reject the $45/month retention offer so the agent can finish the cancellation flow.
tasks.why: The retention offer exceeds the allowed $30/month threshold, so policy requires confirmation before proceeding.

approvals.title: Reject $45/month retention offer and continue cancellation
approvals.reason: The offer is above the current maximum allowed monthly threshold of $30.

timeline.details: Cancellation flow is ready, but the agent is paused because a retention offer needs your decision.

timeline.summary: Found cancellation path and retention offer
timeline.details: The provider portal exposed a $45/month offer before the final cancellation step.
Goal set to cancel service before the next billing cycle closes.

artifacts.name: xfinity-retention-offer.png
artifacts.summary: Screenshot of the portal offer shown immediately before the cancellation confirmation step.

artifacts.name: cancellation-policy-summary.md

chat.content: I found the cancellation path and a $45/month retention offer. I paused because your policy says offers above $30 need approval.

tasks.title: Fight insurance denial
tasks.goal: Prepare an appeal packet and monitor the insurer portal for status changes.
tasks.next_action: Recheck the insurer portal tomorrow morning and upload the physician letter once received.
tasks.why: The physician letter has been requested but has not been uploaded yet.

timeline.details: Denial status is unchanged. Appeal draft remains ready.

tasks.title: Find Seattle flight under $300
tasks.goal: Find 3 strong flight options with aisle seating and acceptable refund terms.
tasks.next_action: No further action required unless the user wants to compare another date range.
tasks.why: Three options under budget were identified and ranked.

timeline.details: Best option is Alaska at $289 with acceptable refund policy and aisle seating available.
Removed flights above $300 and low-value overnight itineraries.

artifacts.name: flight_comparison.png
artifacts.summary: Side-by-side fare and timing comparison for the top 3 options.

chat.content: I found 3 good options under budget. Alaska at $289 is the best fit.
